criteria.label: State the GNB log-score formula and compute class scores on a one-feature toy example.
criteria.example: P(A)=0.6, μ_A=4, σ²_A=1; P(B)=0.4, μ_B=7, σ²_B=2; x=5: show score_A ≈ −1.93 > score_B ≈ −3.33 → predict A.

criteria.label: Explain what GaussianNB estimates during fit and what theta_ and var_ represent.
criteria.example: On a 4-row 2-class 2-feature dataset, fit GaussianNB and print theta_.shape, var_.shape, and match means to hand-computed group statistics.

criteria.label: Contrast GNB with logistic regression on modeling assumptions.
criteria.example: Write one paragraph: GNB models P(x|y) with Gaussian per-feature likelihoods + naive product; logistic regression models P(y|x) with sigmoid — no Gaussian assumption on x.

criteria.label: Identify when the naive independence assumption breaks and propose a mitigation.
criteria.example: Given height and weight strongly correlated within each class, explain double-counting in the product and suggest dropping one feature or using logistic regression / PCA.
